title: carex × subpatula
intro: Carex × subpatula, a hybrid plant of the sedge family (Cyperaceae), is a type of grass-like perennial that is commonly found in wetlands and marshy areas. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Carex × subpatula include its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Carex × subpatula can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
